books.title: The Practice of Network Security Monitoring
books.author: Richard Bejtlich
books.takeaway: NSM methodology — collect, detect, analyse. Changed how I think about what to log and why.

books.title: Threat Modeling: Designing for Security
books.author: Adam Shostack
books.takeaway: STRIDE is just a starting point. The real value is building threat modeling as a habit, not a checklist.

books.title: The Art of Intrusion
books.author: Kevin Mitnick
books.takeaway: Social engineering is always the weakest link. Technical controls mean nothing without awareness training.

books.title: Blue Team Handbook: SOC, SIEM, and Threat Hunting
books.author: Don Murdoch
books.takeaway: Best practical reference for SOC operations. Triage checklists and SIEM query patterns I use regularly.

books.title: CompTIA CySA+ Study Guide
books.author: Mike Chapple & David Seidl
books.takeaway: Currently working through Chapter 7 — Threat Intelligence and Threat Hunting.

books.title: The Web Application Hacker's Handbook
books.author: Stuttard & Pinto
books.takeaway: Reading alongside bug bounty practice. Chapters on authentication flaws and injection are essential.

books.title: NIST Cybersecurity Framework 2.0
books.author: NIST
books.takeaway: The GOVERN function in 2.0 is the biggest addition — risk management now has explicit executive ownership.
